Explanation: This HPLC analysis report demonstrates strong technical execution with well-organized methodology and clear writing. The normalization approach (calculating relative proportions) is mathematically sound and appropriately validated (all proportions sum to 1.0). Strengths include detailed methodology covering preprocessing with Asymmetric Least Squares baseline correction, thoughtful handling of missing metadata through gradient-based elution inference, and appropriate peak detection thresholds. The stacked bar chart effectively visualizes run-to-run comparisons with good categorization (Major/Minor/Trace). However, the analysis lacks depth in investigating the extreme heterogeneity observed (28,000-113,000 mAU·ml ranges, 459-11,000 peak counts). The discrepancy between runs with successful elution inference (2 of 11) versus successful peak detection (all 11) is acknowledged but not thoroughly explored. Data-driven investigation into whether variance reflects biological differences, equipment variability, or methodological limitations would strengthen scientific rigor. The visual presentation is clear and appropriate. Code documentation is comprehensive but verbose for a report setting.
